items.briefing:
A new malicious app called DCloud has been linked to a massive network of scam websites, posing a direct risk to Australian internet users who may encounter fraudulent pages. Separately, a new piece of malware called "Djinn Stealer" is actively targeting cloud and AI service credentials — meaning staff who use tools like Microsoft Azure, AWS, or AI platforms should be on alert for phishing attempts designed to steal their login details.

On a positive note, Google's Global Signal Exchange fraud-sharing initiative is gaining recognition as a model for cross-organisation fraud prevention, which may benefit Australian businesses in time.

**Tip:** Never save cloud service passwords in your browser — use your organisation's approved password manager instead.

featured.title: Why Australian tax agents need to secure every customer login to prevent the next wave of tax fraud
featured.quote: Stolen credentials fuelling tax fraud as attackers bypass ATO controls, directly threatening Australian taxpayers.
